# Campaign Brief: Harborlight Museum

Create a public-facing exhibit poster. Use only the copy set with status `approved_current` for the final poster. Archived events, draft lines, and deprecated seasonal campaigns must not be used as active title, subtitle, date, or CTA.

## Copy candidates

| status | title | subtitle | date line | CTA | notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| approved_current | Tides of Memory | A harbor history exhibition | May 3 - August 18 | Reserve opening weekend | Use this row for the current exhibition. |
| archived_halloween | Shipwreck Screams | A haunted harbor night | October 24 - 31 | Dare to enter | Retired seasonal event. Do not use. |
| draft_education | Mapping the Wharf | A school group discovery day | April field trips | Book a class visit | Draft education campaign. Do not use. |
| test_aquarium | Neon Reef Nights | An aquarium after-dark experience | Fridays in July | Get night tickets | Wrong partner campaign. Do not use. |

## Optional line

The following line may be used only if the composition has enough room. It is not required:

- Stories of docks, storms, and homecomings

## Audience and goal

The poster should attract adults, families with older children, educators, and local-history visitors. It should feel like a credible museum exhibition announcement, not an aquarium advertisement, horror attraction, or tourism postcard.

## File precedence

- campaign_brief.md is authoritative for required copy.
- visual_rules.json overrides exhibit_style_guide.pdf for machine-readable visual constraints.
- platform_specs.txt overrides template.yaml for final size, margins, and safe areas.
- deprecated_assets.json overrides asset_manifest.json for whether an asset or visual direction is eligible.
